behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in algorithm quality at a single function boundary (heuristic vs optimal comparator), not module handoff. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess is required; synthetic coordinate sets are sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: Integration/System were rejected because behavior text is purely about route-length ratio, with no external API or process interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Call the heuristic directly on many deterministic coordinate sets (including adversarial layouts) and compare route length against an exact/optimal solver result for each case; fail if any case exceeds 1.5x.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is about correct computation from two inputs produced by different modules (segment distance + road-type speed config), so handoff/mapping bugs are realistic at module boundaries. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Integration brake checks: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real distance-segment parser/output and speed-configuration module into the estimator, feed a multi-segment route with mixed road types, and assert per-stop ETA/time-window values match expected calculations.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Could be Unit if estimator is fully isolated, but this description explicitly exercises module wiring to catch road-type/config handoff defects.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is arithmetic/threshold logic in one validator (compare total vs remaining hours and compute excess). REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no external infrastructure required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior text does not require cross-module state handoff; it is validator logic.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke validator with total route durations around the boundary (equal, just under, just over) and assert rejection flag plus exact excess amount when over.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at module interaction (geocoder result handling -> filtering -> optimizer input -> skipped-address reporting). REAL-INFRA: although geocoding is an API, the specific failure text is 'returns no results'; this can be reproduced with an in-process stub response, so System is not required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Integration brake checks: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full service pipeline in-process with a stub geocoding adapter that returns empty results for one known address and valid coordinates for others; assert the failed address is reported as skipped and final route excludes it while optimizing remaining stops.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System was considered, but rejected due to concrete behavior text limiting failure mode to semantic API result ('no results') rather than real-network behavior.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: correctness spans deduplication plus downstream route construction ('not visited twice'), so a module boundary failure is plausible. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Integration brake checks: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no.
behaviors.test_description: Execute request normalization + route planning together with input containing duplicate addresses and distinct item lists; assert one stop exists for that address, items are merged correctly, and route includes that stop only once.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit was considered for pure dedupe logic, but behavior explicitly includes downstream visitation semantics, requiring wired modules.
